manufacturing_locations: United States (North Texas - Dallas, Richardson, Sherman)
dependencies: Reliance on third-party foundries and subcontractors for portion of wafer fabrication, assembly and testing
Dependence on third parties to support key portions of operations including manufacturing operations and advanced logic manufacturing process technology development
Key materials, services and utilities required for manufacturing processes and critical manufacturing equipment
Limited number of alternate suppliers for certain materials and services
risks: Suppliers extending lead times, limiting supply or increasing prices due to factors beyond control
Limited or delayed access to and high costs of key materials, services and utilities
Possible shortages of capacity in periods of high demand
Suppliers' inability to develop and deliver advanced logic manufacturing process technology or build facilities in timely, cost-effective manner
Unauthorized disclosure or use of intellectual property by suppliers
Failure by suppliers to fulfill expectations, commitments and responsibilities in accordance with agreed terms or applicable laws

regulatory_risks: Complex laws, rules and regulations affecting domestic and international operations (environment, climate change, safety, health, trade including import/export, bribery and corruption, financial reporting, tax, data privacy and protection, labor and employment, competition, facilities and code compliance, pandemics/epidemics, intellectual property, currency movement)
Compliance costs and restrictions on ability to manufacture products and operate business
Potential litigation, investigations or enforcement activity from government entities regarding compliance
Disruptions to operations or significant fines, penalties or other legal liability
New or expanded laws, rules and regulations or new reporting standards and disclosure requirements
Environmental, social and governance (ESG) reporting, diligence and disclosure requirements
Environmental, safety and health requirements (use of abatement equipment, addition/elimination of materials or processes, costs/fees/reporting on energy/natural resources/materials/gases)
Tax-related changes (changes in tax rates, jurisdictions where profits are taxed, available tax credits/deductions, accounting principles, tariff regulations/surcharges, audit resolutions)
Laws determining how profit is earned and taxed in various jurisdictions (OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting recommendations)
Government incentives subject to reduction, modification, clawback or termination (including CHIPS Act incentives)
Immigration laws and policies affecting ability to recruit internationally or deploy employees

sanction_risks: Geopolitical tensions affecting global trade through tariffs, import/export restrictions, trade embargoes and sanctions, restrictions on cross-border investment
Trade barriers impacting ability to deliver products, support customers, receive manufacturing equipment
Customers seeking alternate suppliers due to geopolitical tensions
China actively promoting and reshaping domestic semiconductor industry through policy changes and investment, potentially restricting participation in China market or preventing effective competition
